question: O que read faz em C?

after.title: read() overview
after.snippet: read() (declared in <unistd.h>) is the POSIX syscall that copies up to nbyte bytes from the file associated with the open file descriptor fildes into the buffer buf and returns how many bytes were actually transferred. The prototype `ssize_t read(int fildes, void *buf, size_t nbyte);` mirrors pread() except that it adv...

justification: A nova visão geral do syscall aparece no topo e descreve assinatura, cópia de bytes e retorno, cobrindo a pergunta diretamente.
